title_updated: GPT-5.4 Thinking: Launch frontier model combining reasoning, coding & agentic workflows
content_updated:
OpenAI releases GPT-5.4 Thinking, a frontier model that unifies advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows into a single system, incorporating the coding capabilities of GPT-5.3-Codex while improving performance across tools, spreadsheets, presentations, and documents.
In ChatGPT, GPT-5.4 Thinking now provides an upfront plan of its reasoning so users can adjust course mid-response, improving deep web research for specific queries and maintaining better context during extended thinking sessions.
GPT-5.4 builds on the GPT-5 series' iterative improvement pattern, succeeding GPT-5.3 as the model that gets complex real-world work done with less back-and-forth interaction required from the user.

title_updated: Codex App: Launch Windows desktop client for parallel coding agents
content_updated:
The Codex app is now available on Windows for ChatGPT plans that include Codex, providing a desktop surface for running multiple Codex agents in parallel with isolated worktrees and reviewable diffs.
Users can sign in with their ChatGPT account and manage tasks across the app, CLI, and IDE without switching tools, with the ability to edit, discard, or turn diffs into pull requests directly.
This expands Codex's platform availability beyond macOS, which launched in February 2026, bringing the same parallel-agent workflow to Windows users.

title_updated: GPT-5.3 Instant: Improve accuracy, web search & conversational flow
content_updated:
GPT-5.3 Instant delivers more accurate answers, richer web search results with better contextualization, and reduces unnecessary dead ends, caveats, and overly declarative phrasing that interrupt conversational flow.
This update focuses on everyday ChatGPT experience qualities like tone, relevance, and conversational flow, addressing nuanced issues that do not always appear in benchmarks but significantly shape whether the tool feels helpful.
GPT-5.3 Instant directly reflects accumulated user feedback, continuing the iterative improvement cadence within the GPT-5 model generation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Web & Android: Add image prompt editing, faster sharing & more
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces multiple updates across Web and Android, including the ability to edit messages with image attachments on Web, opening search results in new tabs, faster sharing, and exportable Code Block visuals like flowcharts and diagrams.
Android improvements include automatic scrolling to the latest message, faster sidebar actions, improved dictation visuals, and automatic keyboard opening after file attachment, alongside bug fixes for GPT-5.2 Thinking streaming errors and Voice Mode button flickering.
This is a bundled release addressing everyday usability across both platforms, with Web receiving four feature updates and Android receiving four UX enhancements plus three bug fixes.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Add sources from apps, chats & ad-hoc text
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now lets users build a living knowledge base by adding sources from connected apps like Slack and Google Drive, saving useful ChatGPT responses from chats, and pasting ad-hoc text such as notes, briefs, or reference material.
Users can paste links to Slack channels or Google Drive files to add them directly as project sources, and connected apps can be used directly in chats for seamless context integration.
This update transforms Projects from a static file repository into a dynamic knowledge base that captures context from wherever it already lives, reducing the friction of manually uploading documents.

title_updated: ChatGPT Thinking: Expand context window to 256k tokens (128k in, 128k out)
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports a total context window of 256,000 tokens when Thinking mode is manually selected, split evenly between 128,000 input tokens and 128,000 maximum output tokens.
This represents a significant increase from the previous 196,000 total tokens, giving users more room for both complex inputs and extended model reasoning in a single session.
The expanded context is available when users manually select Thinking mode, enabling longer and more detailed analyses for complex tasks that require extended reasoning chains.

title_updated: Code Blocks: Launch interactive write, edit & preview in ChatGPT
content_updated:
ChatGPT Code Blocks have been upgraded to be fully interactive, allowing users to write, edit, and preview code all in one place within the chat interface.
Users can now write and edit text in-line, preview diagrams and mini apps directly in chat, and review code in split-screen views without leaving the conversation.
This update transforms Code Blocks from a read-only display into an interactive development environment, reducing the need to switch between ChatGPT and external editors for quick coding tasks.

title_updated: Legacy Models: Execute retirement of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, o4-mini & GPT-5 Instant/Thinking
content_updated:
OpenAI has retired GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, OpenAI o4-mini, GPT-5 Instant, and GPT-5 Thinking from ChatGPT as previously announced, completing the legacy model cleanup.
API access to these models remains unchanged; the retirement applies only to the ChatGPT consumer product, with users having been migrated to GPT-5.2 as the successor model.
This follows the January 29 advance notice and completes the transition announced two weeks prior, consolidating ChatGPT around the GPT-5.2 model family.

title_updated: ChatGPT Updates: Expand file uploads to 20, add file types & improve long chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports uploading up to 20 files in a single message on Web, up from 10, with expanded file type support for text, code, source files, logs, and configuration files.
Additional improvements include cleaner Cmd+A selection behavior on Web, quick tools in the Android composer, a streamlined Android message composer, iOS performance enhancements, and improved reliability for long Thinking chats across all platforms.
This bundled release addresses productivity across Web, iOS, and Android, with the file upload expansion and new format support being the most impactful changes for users working with multiple documents.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve instruction following & web search for free-tier users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice receives an update that improves its ability to follow user instructions and use tools like web search to provide better responses for free-tier and rate-limited Plus users.
This update targets the standard Voice mode used primarily by free ChatGPT users and by Plus users when limits are reached for the main model, rather than the Advanced Voice mode.
The improvement continues OpenAI's pattern of incremental Voice quality updates, following similar instruction-following fixes deployed for paid users in January 2026.

title_updated: Deep Research: Launch editable research plans, focused sources & fullscreen reports
content_updated:
ChatGPT Deep Research introduces improvements for more accurate and credible reports with greater user control, including the ability to focus research on specific websites and connected apps as trusted sources.
A redesigned sidebar entry point and fullscreen report view make it easier to start, review, and manage research, with the ability to create and edit a research plan before it begins and adjust direction mid-run.
Rolling out to Plus and Pro users first, with Free and Go users following in coming days, this update represents a significant step toward user-directed research with real-time course correction.

title_updated: GPT-5.2 Instant: Improve response style, tone & answer relevance
content_updated:
GPT-5.2 Instant receives an update in ChatGPT and the API that improves response style and quality, delivering more measured and grounded tone that is contextually appropriate to conversations.
The model now produces clearer, more relevant answers to advice-seeking and how-to questions, more reliably placing the most important information upfront in responses.
This continues the iterative refinement of the GPT-5.2 generation, following the December 2025 launch and focusing on the qualitative aspects of response delivery that shape everyday user experience.

title_updated: ChatGPT Ads: Begin ad test for Free & Go tier users in the U.S.
content_updated:
OpenAI begins testing advertisements in ChatGPT for logged-in adult users on the Free and Go tiers in the United States, with Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education plans excluded from the test.
Ads are contextually shown using information that stays within ChatGPT, including current conversation topics, interaction history, and past chats with memories, and are always clearly labeled as sponsored and visually separated from organic answers. Users can dismiss ads, share feedback, delete ad data, and manage personalization at any time.
This is OpenAI's first public ad experiment in ChatGPT, potentially opening a new revenue stream beyond subscriptions, with safeguards excluding sensitive topics like health, mental health, and politics, and excluding users under 18.

title_updated: GPT-5.2 Thinking: Restore Extended thinking level & update thinking time settings
content_updated:
OpenAI restores the Extended thinking level for GPT-5.2 Thinking to its prior setting, correcting an inadvertent reduction from January, while also making a small reduction to Standard thinking time based on testing.
The timeline spans from January 10 when Standard and Light thinking times were first lowered, through February 3 with another Standard reduction, to February 4 when Extended was restored to its original level.
OpenAI periodically adjusts default thinking time to balance answer quality and response speed, with the thinking level toggle introduced in September 2025 giving users explicit control over reasoning depth for their questions.

title_updated: Codex App: Launch macOS desktop client for parallel coding agents
content_updated:
OpenAI releases the Codex app for macOS, a command center for managing multiple coding agents in parallel with isolated worktrees, reviewable diffs, reusable skills, and automation capabilities.
The app is available with ChatGPT plans that include Codex, with a limited-time promotion giving Free and Go users included limits and Plus and Pro users doubled Codex rate limits.
This marks the first dedicated desktop application for Codex, moving it beyond the CLI and web interfaces into a standalone productivity tool for developers managing complex, long-horizon coding tasks.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch visual responses with inline highlights & side panels
content_updated:
ChatGPT responses are now more visual and easier to scan, with at-a-glance visuals for everyday questions like checking stats, converting units, or quick calculations, plus highlighted key entities that users can tap for more context.
Tapping any highlighted person, place, product, or idea opens a side panel with key facts and trusted sources, enabling users to understand context without asking follow-up questions.
Rolling out globally on iOS, Android, and web, this update represents a shift toward richer, more scannable responses that reduce the need for follow-up queries.

title_updated: Legacy Models: Announce retirement of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1 & o4-mini effective Feb 13
content_updated:
OpenAI announces that GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and o4-mini will be retired from ChatGPT on February 13, 2026, alongside the previously announced GPT-5 Instant and Thinking retirement.
API access to these models remains unchanged; the retirement applies only to the ChatGPT consumer product, with users to be automatically migrated to GPT-5.2 as the successor.
This advance notice gives users two weeks to prepare; a follow-up execution on February 13 will confirm the completion of the retirement.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve search response quality & shopping results
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice now delivers more complete and up-to-date search responses, including better access to shopping results when users ask voice queries that involve product or purchase information.
The improvement targets the quality and completeness of web-sourced answers delivered through the Voice interface, making spoken responses more informative and actionable.
This update builds on ongoing Voice mode improvements, continuing to close the quality gap between text-based and voice-based search experiences in ChatGPT.

title_updated: GPT-5.2 Instant: Update default personality for more conversational tone
content_updated:
GPT-5.2 Instant's default personality is updated to be more conversational and better at adapting its tone contextually, making exchanges feel smoother and more natural.
Users can still select a different base style and tone through the Personalization menu in settings, along with tuning characteristics like warmth and emoji use for full control over the experience.
This personality update follows the pattern of iterative tone refinements across the GPT-5 series, building on the warmer personality introduced with GPT-5 in August 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve instruction following & fix custom instruction echo bug
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice now better follows user instructions and resolves a bug where custom instructions were sometimes repeated back verbatim during voice conversations.
This update targets the Voice mode for paid users and does not affect Advanced Voice mode or the free-tier voice experience.
This is part of ongoing Voice improvements following the November 2025 integration of Voice into the main chat interface.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Roll out age prediction model to apply teen safeguards
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out an age prediction model on ChatGPT consumer plans to determine whether an account likely belongs to someone under 18, enabling appropriate safeguards and experiences for teens.
The model analyzes behavioral and account-level signals including account age, typical activity times, usage patterns over time, and the user's stated age to make its predictions.
Adults incorrectly placed in the under-18 experience can verify their age via a selfie through Persona, a verification service available in Settings, ensuring the system is correctable while prioritizing teen safety.

title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Improve detail retrieval from past chats for Plus & Pro
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now more reliably find specific details from past chats when reference chat history is enabled, with any past chat used to answer a question appearing as a clickable source for review.
This memory improvement is available globally for Plus and Pro users, building on the enhanced memory infrastructure that references conversation history for more relevant responses.
The update makes ChatGPT's long-term memory more precise and transparent, with source attribution allowing users to verify and revisit the original context behind memory-informed answers.

title_updated: ChatGPT Dictation: Reduce empty transcriptions & improve accuracy
content_updated:
ChatGPT's dictation capabilities are improved for all logged-in users, significantly reducing empty transcriptions and improving overall transcription accuracy.
The update targets the voice-to-text input feature used in the message composer, making it more reliable for users who prefer speaking over typing.
This improvement addresses a common pain point where dictation would occasionally produce blank results, enhancing the reliability of voice input across all ChatGPT plans.

title_updated: ChatGPT Health: Launch dedicated health & wellness space with medical records
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Health, a dedicated space for health and wellness conversations that securely connects to medical records, Apple Health, and supported wellness apps so answers can be grounded in the user's own data.
Health appears as a new sidebar space with conversations, memory, and files kept separate from the rest of ChatGPT and not used for model training. At launch, it is available on web and iOS with Android coming soon for Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users in supported countries.
This marks ChatGPT's first dedicated vertical feature for healthcare, rolling out gradually to a small group of early users with a waitlist, as part of OpenAI's push into domain-specific experiences.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Your Year with ChatGPT 2025 retrospective experience
content_updated:
OpenAI launches Your Year with ChatGPT, an optional personalized end-of-year experience reflecting on how users interacted with ChatGPT in 2025, including high-level conversation themes and usage statistics.
Available to Free, Plus, and Pro users in the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, plus Go, Plus, and Pro users in India, it requires Memory and Reference Chat History to be enabled with a minimum activity threshold.
This marks ChatGPT's first annual retrospective feature, similar to year-in-review experiences from other platforms, rolling out gradually in English and excluded from Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans.

title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Add granular characteristic controls for warmth & style
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers detailed characteristic controls beyond base style and tone presets, allowing users to individually adjust warmth, enthusiasm, use of headers and lists, and emoji frequency.
These controls are accessible from the Personalization pane in settings and work alongside existing presets like candid and friendly for more fine-grained control over response style.
This extends the personalization system launched earlier in 2025, giving users more precise control over how ChatGPT communicates rather than relying solely on preset tone profiles.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch app directory for browsing & adding third-party apps
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces an app directory where users can browse and add approved apps that work with tools and data directly inside conversations, from interactive in-chat experiences to secure third-party data connections.
Existing connectors now appear in the directory as apps, consolidating tool management in one place, and developers can submit apps for review and publication to reach the broader ChatGPT user base.
Apps are available to all logged-in users with availability varying by plan and region, marking a significant step toward ChatGPT as a platform for third-party integrations beyond the earlier GPT Store concept.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch pinned chats for quick access across all platforms
content_updated:
ChatGPT now lets users pin chats to the top of their conversation list for quick access to important conversations, available on iOS, Android, Web, and Atlas.
On web, users hover over a chat in the sidebar and select Pin chat from the menu; on mobile, a long-press on a chat reveals the pin option.
This addresses a frequently requested feature for users managing many conversations, making it easier to keep key discussions accessible without scrolling through history.

title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Move task management into Pulse for Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Tasks, which let users set up automated prompts that run at scheduled times, can now be managed directly within Pulse alongside creation, editing, and review capabilities.
Users can create tasks in chat or in Pulse and edit them anytime after creation, with all tasks visible in one centralized location within the Pulse interface.
This consolidation is exclusive to ChatGPT Pro users, integrating the previously separate Tasks feature into the Pulse dashboard launched in September 2025 for a more unified workflow experience.

title_updated: ChatGPT Images: Launch improved image generation on Web & Mobile (iOS & Android)
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a new and improved image generation experience powered by its best model yet, with precise instruction following and editing capabilities that make AI image creation more useful for everyday edits and creative transformations.
All generated images are automatically saved to My Images at chatgpt.com/images, providing a single place to browse, revisit, and reuse work without searching through past conversations.
Available on Free, Go, Plus, Edu, and Pro plans with Business and Enterprise coming soon, this update represents a major upgrade to ChatGPT's visual creation capabilities.

title_updated: ChatGPT macOS: Announce Voice retirement effective January 15, 2026
content_updated:
OpenAI announces the retirement of the Voice experience in the ChatGPT macOS desktop app, effective January 15, 2026, to focus on more unified voice experiences across other platforms.
Voice will continue to be available on chatgpt.com, iOS, Android, and the Windows app, with no other ChatGPT features on macOS affected by this change.
This retirement consolidates voice functionality onto platforms where it can be maintained and improved more effectively, following the November 2025 unification of Voice into the main chat interface.

title_updated: GPT-5.2: Launch smarter GPT-5 series upgrade with Instant, Thinking & Pro variants
content_updated:
OpenAI releases GPT-5.2 as the next upgrade in the GPT-5 series, offering improved intelligence for work and learning across three variants: Instant for everyday tasks, Thinking for harder problems, and Pro for the most difficult questions.
GPT-5.2 Instant improves info-seeking, how-tos, technical writing, and translation while keeping a conversational tone; Thinking excels at spreadsheet formatting, financial modeling, and coding; Pro delivers fewer major errors across complex domains. All three models have a new knowledge cutoff of August 2025.
For free and Go users, automatic model switching for reasoning is removed, with GPT-5.2 Instant as the default and Thinking available as an explicit choice, giving users more control over model selection.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Integrate voice into main chat interface with text & visual responses
content_updated:
ChatGPT Voice is redesigned as a seamless part of the main chat interface, allowing users to speak, listen, and see conversations simultaneously without requiring a separate mode.
Answers are spoken alongside streamed text, image search results, and widgets like maps and weather in the same chat thread, enabling users to follow typed answers while listening, see visual results in real time, type back when unable to talk, and share photos for voice discussion.
Rolling out globally on mobile and chatgpt.com, users who prefer the original experience can switch back via Settings by enabling Separate mode, while this integration marks a major step toward unified multimodal interactions.

title_updated: ChatGPT Shopping: Launch interactive research for personalized buying guides
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Shopping Research, an interactive experience where users describe their needs and ChatGPT asks clarifying questions, researches the internet, reviews quality sources, and builds a personalized buyer's guide with options, tradeoffs, and purchase links.
The experience leverages memory from past conversations and allows real-time feedback to guide research, available on Web, iOS, and Android for logged-in Free, Go, Plus, and Pro users with nearly unlimited usage through the holiday season.
This represents ChatGPT's first dedicated shopping workflow, going beyond simple product search to provide an end-to-end research assistant that personalizes recommendations based on user preferences and constraints.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Add more inline web images for people, places & products
content_updated:
ChatGPT now includes more inline images from the web alongside relevant paragraphs to help users quickly understand topics involving well-known people, places, and products, with clickable images showing original dimensions and source attribution.
The improvements are gradually rolling out globally across all ChatGPT plans on web, iOS, and Android for responses using GPT-5.1, adding visual clarity where images can enhance understanding.
This builds on the broader push toward more visual and scannable responses, making ChatGPT answers feel more like rich reference pages than plain text outputs.

title_updated: Instant Checkout: Expand to Shopify merchants starting with Spanx, Skims & Glossier
content_updated:
ChatGPT Instant Checkout now supports purchases from Shopify merchants, starting with Spanx, Skims, and Glossier, allowing users to complete purchases directly in chat with a quick confirmation of shipping and payment details.
Instant Checkout is available for logged-in Plus, Pro, and Free users in the US, with the purchase flow triggered when a product from a supported Shopify merchant is available.
This expansion follows the initial Etsy launch in September 2025, bringing over one million Shopify merchants into the Instant Checkout ecosystem and significantly broadening the range of products available for in-chat purchasing.

title_updated: GPT-5.1 Pro: Launch upgraded model with improved clarity & relevance
content_updated:
OpenAI updates GPT-5 Pro to GPT-5.1 Pro, with early testers consistently preferring it over GPT-5 Pro and rating it especially highly for writing help, data science, and business questions due to improved clarity, relevance, and structure.
GPT-5.1 Pro is rolling out to all ChatGPT Pro users and available in the model picker, while GPT-5 Pro remains accessible as a legacy model for 90 days before retirement.
This follows the broader GPT-5.1 upgrade pattern, bringing the Pro tier's most capable model in line with the Instant and Thinking variants that were updated earlier in November 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch group chats pilot in New Zealand, Japan, South Korea & Taiwan
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a pilot of group chats, allowing multiple people and ChatGPT to collaborate in the same conversation for trip planning, decision-making, and project work while ChatGPT helps search, summarize, and generate content.
The pilot is rolling out on ChatGPT Web, iOS, and Android in New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan, with early feedback informing how the feature expands to more regions and plans over time.
This marks ChatGPT's first real-time collaborative feature, moving beyond single-user interactions toward a shared workspace model where multiple participants can leverage AI assistance simultaneously.

title_updated: GPT-5.1: Launch smarter Instant & Thinking models with adaptive reasoning
content_updated:
OpenAI updates GPT-5 to GPT-5.1 Instant and GPT-5.1 Thinking, with Instant now using light adaptive reasoning for tougher questions while staying fast, and Thinking adapting its reasoning time more precisely for complex tasks with clearer, less jargony responses.
GPT-5.1 Auto continues routing queries to the best model, and GPT-5 models remain available for three months under Legacy models. Rolling out first to paid users, then to free and logged-out users, with Enterprise and Edu getting temporary early-access toggles.
GPT-5.1 will also be available in the API as gpt-5.1-chat-latest for Instant and gpt-5.1 for Thinking, continuing the rapid iterative improvement cadence within the GPT-5 generation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Launch updated tone presets & granular style controls
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates its tone controls with new presets including Default, Friendly, Efficient, Professional, Candid, and Quirky, while introducing experimental granular controls over conciseness, warmth, scannability, and emoji frequency.
Changes made in Personalization now apply immediately across all chats including existing conversations, and GPT-5.1 models follow custom instructions and style preferences more reliably than before.
This represents a significant expansion of user control over ChatGPT's communication style, building on the personality system introduced with GPT-5 and making personalization changes instant rather than requiring new conversations.

title_updated: ChatGPT Personalization: Apply personality & custom instructions to all existing chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT now applies changes to personality and custom instructions immediately across all chats, including existing conversations, rather than only new conversations as was previously the case.
This update ensures a consistent experience everywhere without requiring users to start new threads when they change their personalization settings.
Previously, users had to start new conversations to see personalization changes take effect, creating an inconsistent experience across old and new chats that this update resolves.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch mid-query interruption for deep research & long-running tasks
content_updated:
ChatGPT now allows users to interrupt long-running queries like deep research or GPT-5 Pro to refine their question mid-stream without restarting or losing progress.
Users click an update button in the sidebar to send new information as a message, and the model adjusts its approach based on the new requirements, enabling real-time course correction during extended reasoning sessions.
This feature addresses a common frustration with long-running queries where users realize mid-way that they need to add constraints or change direction, previously requiring a complete restart.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch 12-month free promotion for eligible users in India
content_updated:
Eligible customers in India can redeem 12 months of ChatGPT Go at no cost on ChatGPT web or Android app via Google Play, with the Apple App Store option arriving the following week and no promo code required.
The promotion is available to new users, free plan users, and current ChatGPT Go subscribers in India, limited to one redemption per account and requiring a payment method such as credit card or UPI.
This promotion targets the Indian market where ChatGPT Go originally launched in August 2025, aiming to accelerate adoption in one of OpenAI's key growth regions. The promo ended as of January 21, 2026.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 8 additional European countries, reaching 98 total
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go expands to eight additional European countries including Austria, Czech Republic, Denmark, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Spain, and Sweden, bringing total availability to 98 countries.
The low-cost subscription plan provides more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan, available across web, mobile, and desktop.
This European expansion follows the October rollout to 71 additional countries and continues OpenAI's strategy of making an affordable paid tier available globally.

title_updated: Codex & Sora: Launch credits for flexible usage beyond included limits
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces purchasable credits for additional usage in Codex and Sora, allowing users who hit their included limits to seamlessly buy more directly within the Codex dashboard or Sora app.
Credits are available for ChatGPT Free, Go, Plus, and Pro plans, providing a pay-as-you-go option that extends beyond the fixed rate limits included with each subscription tier.
This marks OpenAI's first usage-based billing model alongside subscriptions, creating a hybrid monetization approach for compute-intensive features like coding agents and video generation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Launch on Web & Atlas with daily asynchronous research summaries
content_updated:
ChatGPT Pulse is now available on web and rolling out to Atlas for Pro users, enabling asynchronous daily research based on past chats, memory, and feedback, delivered as visual summaries the next day.
Users can scan summaries at a glance, expand for more detail, save for later, or ask follow-up questions, with Pulse synthesizing information proactively based on the user's context and interests.
This web launch follows the initial iOS and Android release in September 2025, expanding Pulse's proactive intelligence feature to desktop users for a more complete cross-platform experience.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch low-cost subscription plan in Brazil
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go launches in Brazil, offering an affordable subscription that includes more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, matching the feature set of Go plans in other markets.
Brazil becomes the latest market for ChatGPT Go as OpenAI continues its global expansion of the low-cost tier, following launches in India, Indonesia, and dozens of other countries throughout 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Launch shared projects with collaboration for all plans
content_updated:
ChatGPT shared projects are now available to all users including Free, Plus, Pro, and Go plans globally on web, iOS, and Android, enabling group collaboration where ChatGPT draws from shared chats, files, and custom instructions.
Collaboration limits vary by plan: Pro supports up to 40 files and 100 collaborators, Plus and Go support 25 files and 10 collaborators, and Free supports 5 files and 5 collaborators, with project owners controlling visibility settings.
Shared projects enable use cases like group work, content creation, reporting, and research, representing ChatGPT's first team collaboration feature available across all subscription tiers.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Upgrade signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant model
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates the default model for signed-out users to GPT-5 Instant, giving more people access to higher-quality responses without requiring account creation or login.
This change applies the same GPT-5 Instant model used by logged-in free users to the signed-out experience, removing the quality gap between authenticated and unauthenticated usage.
The upgrade continues OpenAI's strategy of democratizing access to its best models, following the pattern of making each new model generation the default for progressively wider audiences.

title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Launch automatic memory management for Plus & Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT now automatically manages saved memories by keeping the most relevant details prioritized and moving less important ones to the background, preventing the memory full state that previously required manual cleanup.
The system considers factors like recency and topic frequency to decide which memories stay top of mind, with users able to search, sort, prioritize, deprioritize, and restore prior versions of saved memories.
Rolling out to Plus and Pro users globally on web, this update makes memory management seamless while keeping users in control, with the ability to turn off automatic management at any time.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 71 additional countries, reaching 89 total
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go expands to 71 additional countries, bringing total availability to 89 countries, with the affordable subscription offering more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, though iOS app subscriptions are not available in Cambodia, Laos, and Nepal where web and Android remain available.
This massive expansion significantly broadens ChatGPT Go's global reach following its initial launch in India in August 2025 and subsequent single-country rollouts.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Slack connector & Slack app integration for Plus, Pro & Business
content_updated:
OpenAI launches two Slack integrations: a connector that brings Slack context into ChatGPT conversations, and a ChatGPT app for Slack that enables direct interaction with ChatGPT from within the Slack sidebar.
The Slack app supports one-on-one chats, thread summarization, action item extraction, reply drafting, and message search, with chats synced to the ChatGPT sidebar for cross-platform continuity. The connector enables secure context from channels and DMs in chat and Deep Research.
Both integrations are available to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise/Edu customers, with the Slack app requiring a paid Slack account and the connector required before installing the app.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add Notion & Linear as synced connectors for Pro users
content_updated:
Pro users can now connect Notion and Linear as synced connectors, enabling ChatGPT to automatically reference indexed Notion pages and Linear issues and discussions when relevant in conversations.
Users enable sync from Connector settings after connecting to Notion or Linear, and ChatGPT will automatically reference the indexed content without manual selection for each query.
This adds two popular productivity tools to the connector ecosystem, expanding Pro users' ability to ground ChatGPT responses in their actual project data and documentation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Expand to 16 additional countries with affordable subscription plan
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go expands to 16 additional countries, offering more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan at an affordable price.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, though iOS app subscriptions are not available in Cambodia, Laos, and Nepal where web and Android remain options.
This continues the gradual global rollout of ChatGPT's low-cost subscription tier following its initial launch in India and subsequent expansion to Indonesia and Brazil.

title_updated: Codex: Launch Slack integration, SDK & updated rate limits
content_updated:
Codex rolls out new capabilities including Slack integration for team workflows, programmatic control through the Codex SDK, and updated rate limits for managing usage.
The Slack integration allows developers to interact with Codex directly from their team communication tool, while the SDK enables automated and programmatic task management beyond the web and CLI interfaces.
These updates position Codex as a more team-oriented and automatable development tool, expanding its reach beyond individual developer workflows into broader team collaboration and CI/CD-style automation.

title_updated: GPT-5 Instant: Improve mental distress detection & crisis response
content_updated:
GPT-5 Instant is updated to better recognize and support people in moments of distress, with improved detection and response to potential signs of mental and emotional distress guided by mental health experts.
GPT-5 Instant now performs as well as GPT-5 Thinking on sensitive conversations, and the real-time router directs distress-related parts of conversations to GPT-5 Instant even when a non-reasoning model is selected.
This safety update reflects ongoing work to make ChatGPT's responses supportive and grounding during crisis moments, building on research shared in OpenAI's blog about reducing sycophancy while maintaining warmth.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch parental controls for teen account management globally
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out parental controls globally in ChatGPT, allowing parents or guardians to connect accounts with teens and manage settings including content protections, feature access, time limits, and usage safeguards.
Parents can set quiet hours, disable voice mode, turn off memory, remove image generation, and opt out of model training, while connected teens automatically receive additional content protections that only parents can disable.
In rare cases where safety systems detect possible signs of serious risk, parents may be notified with limited information needed to support their teen's safety, with notification preferences configurable via email, SMS, or push notification.

title_updated: Instant Checkout: Launch in-chat purchasing with Etsy via Agentic Commerce Protocol
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces Instant Checkout in ChatGPT powered by the Agentic Commerce Protocol built with Stripe, enabling users to find products and complete purchases directly in chat starting with US Etsy sellers.
Instant Checkout ranks merchants by inventory, price, quality, and primary seller status, with payment going directly from user to merchant. Plus, Pro, and Free users in the US can use the feature, while over one million Shopify merchants are coming soon.
The Agentic Commerce Protocol provides merchants and developers with tools to build agentic commerce experiences connecting to ChatGPT's 700 million weekly active users, with documentation and example implementations available on GitHub.

title_updated: ChatGPT Pulse: Launch proactive daily research summaries for Pro users on iOS & Android
content_updated:
ChatGPT launches Pulse as an early preview for Pro users on iOS and Android, delivering personalized daily updates based on asynchronous research that synthesizes information from memory, chat history, and direct feedback.
Updates are delivered as visual summaries users can scan, expand, or dive deeper into with follow-up questions, with feedback via thumbs up or down and a curate feature to shape future content. New requests made in the evening aim to appear the next morning.
As a preview, Pulse has limitations and may occasionally show irrelevant suggestions, but it improves as it learns from real use. It requires saved memory and chat history to be enabled and can be switched off anytime in settings.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch low-cost subscription in Indonesia at Rp 75,000/month
content_updated:
ChatGPT Go launches in Indonesia at Rp 75,000 per month, offering more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan.
The plan is available across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows, matching the feature set available in India where ChatGPT Go originally launched.
Indonesia becomes the second country to receive ChatGPT Go following India's August 2025 launch, as OpenAI continues expanding its affordable subscription tier to high-growth markets.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Improve quality & latency for GPT-4o mini-powered Advanced Voice
content_updated:
OpenAI improves the quality and reduces the latency of responses in the version of Advanced Voice Mode powered by GPT-4o mini, making voice interactions faster and more responsive.
The update targets the GPT-4o mini voice backend specifically, which serves as a lighter-weight option within the Advanced Voice experience.
This is part of ongoing incremental improvements to Voice Mode performance following the broader Advanced Voice upgrades announced in June 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch thinking time toggle for Plus, Business & Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces a thinking time toggle in the message composer for GPT-5 with Thinking, giving users control over reasoning depth with Standard and Extended options for Plus and Business users, plus Light and Heavy options for Pro users.
Standard is the new default balancing speed and intelligence, with Extended providing the previous default reasoning depth. All preferences are saved for future queries, and the toggle is available on chatgpt.com with mobile support coming soon.
This addresses user feedback that GPT-5 Thinking answers sometimes take longer than desired, letting users decide between quicker answers and deeper reasoning based on question complexity.

title_updated: ChatGPT Search: Improve factuality, shopping detection & answer formatting
content_updated:
ChatGPT Search receives further improvements making results more accurate, reliable, and useful, with fewer hallucinations improving overall answer quality.
Shopping intent detection is improved so products appear when wanted and results stay focused otherwise, while answer formatting is enhanced for quick understanding without losing detail or quality.
These search improvements build on ChatGPT's ongoing effort to make its web search capabilities competitive with dedicated search engines for everyday information needs.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Move personalization settings from modal into main Settings pane
content_updated:
Personalization settings no longer open in a separate modal and are now accessible directly within Settings under the Personalization section, including personality selection, custom instructions, and Memory management.
This UI change consolidates personalization controls into the main settings flow, making them more discoverable and consistent with other ChatGPT configuration options.
The move simplifies the settings experience by eliminating the separate modal that previously handled personalization, reducing the number of different interfaces users need to navigate.

title_updated: Codex: Launch GPT-5-Codex model optimized for agentic coding tasks
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces GPT-5-Codex, a GPT-5 variant optimized for agentic coding, available as the default for cloud tasks and code review in Codex and selectable for local workflows via the Codex CLI and IDE extension.
GPT-5-Codex is designed for coding-focused work in Codex environments while GPT-5 remains recommended for general non-coding tasks. The model is not currently supported in ChatGPT or the API.
This specialized model represents OpenAI's investment in purpose-built coding models for the Codex platform, separate from the general-purpose GPT-5 series available in ChatGPT.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Delay Standard Voice retirement & keep it available
content_updated:
OpenAI postpones the retirement of Standard Voice Mode, originally planned after a 30-day sunset following the August 2025 Advanced Voice expansion, keeping it available while addressing user feedback about Advanced Voice.
The decision responds to feedback that Standard Voice is valued by some users, and OpenAI wants to ensure the transition feels right before proceeding with deprecation.
Advanced Voice was recently expanded from minutes per day to hours for Free users and near-unlimited for Plus users, but the quality gap between Standard and Advanced Voice prompted this pause.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch conversation branching on web for all logged-in users
content_updated:
ChatGPT now supports branching conversations on web, allowing users to explore different directions from any message without losing the original thread by hovering over a message and selecting Branch in new chat.
The feature creates a separate conversation starting from the selected message point, preserving the original chat while enabling parallel exploration of alternative approaches or topics.
Available today for all logged-in web users, this addresses a common workflow need where users want to try different approaches from the same starting point without duplicating or losing their original conversation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Expand to Free tier with up to 5 file uploads per project
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects are now available on the Free tier with up to 5 file uploads per project, while paid tiers receive updated limits: Plus, Go, and Edu support 25 files, and Pro, Business, and Enterprise support 40 files per project.
New customization options including colors and icons are available for all plans to help keep projects organized and visually distinguishable.
This expansion makes Projects accessible to all ChatGPT users for the first time, following the initial launch for paid users, with the Free tier receiving a limited but functional version of the organizational feature.

title_updated: Codex: Launch IDE extension, ChatGPT sign-in & local-cloud handoff
content_updated:
Codex expands to work everywhere developers build with a new VS Code/Cursor IDE extension, ChatGPT sign-in eliminating API key setup, seamless local-to-cloud handoff, an upgraded CLI with refreshed UI, and automated GitHub PR code reviews.
The IDE extension brings Codex into VS Code, Cursor, and other VS Code forks for previewing local changes and editing code, while the local-to-cloud handoff lets developers pair locally then delegate tasks to the cloud asynchronously.
All Codex product information and updates are moving to a new dedicated developers site at developers.openai.com/codex, signaling the maturation of Codex from a feature into a full developer platform.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Launch project-only memory for focused, self-contained workspaces
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now offer a project-only memory option that creates a focused, self-contained workspace where ChatGPT uses only conversations within that project for context and does not reference or carry information to or from outside the project.
Personal Memory must be enabled to use this feature, which is initially available on the ChatGPT website and Windows app, with iOS, Android, and macOS app support following in coming weeks.
This feature is useful for long-running or sensitive work where users want ChatGPT anchored to a specific project's tone, context, and history without cross-contamination from other conversations.

title_updated: ChatGPT Go: Launch low-cost subscription in India at Rs 399/month
content_updated:
OpenAI launches ChatGPT Go in India at Rs 399 per month including GST, a new low-cost subscription plan offering more messages, larger file uploads, expanded image generation, advanced data analysis, and longer memory beyond the Free plan.
ChatGPT Go is geo-restricted to India at launch and available via credit card or UPI payment, accessible across web, mobile on iOS and Android, and desktop on macOS and Windows.
This is OpenAI's first affordable subscription tier designed for price-sensitive markets, targeting India's large user base as the initial launch country before planned expansion to other regions.

title_updated: GPT-5: Update default personality to be warmer & more approachable
content_updated:
OpenAI makes GPT-5's default personality warmer and more familiar in response to user feedback that the initial version came across as too reserved and professional, with small acknowledgements like brief recognitions of user circumstances.
The warmth improvements are distinct from sycophancy, defined as excessive flattery, and internal evaluations confirm no increase in sycophantic behavior compared to the previous GPT-5 personality.
Teaching models to be warm without being sycophantic remains an ongoing research challenge, with this update representing OpenAI's first personality adjustment to GPT-5 since its August 2025 launch.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Expand Gmail, Google Calendar & Contacts to Plus users
content_updated:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts connectors are now available to ChatGPT Plus users globally, expanding access that was previously limited to Pro users since August 12, 2025.
Once enabled, ChatGPT automatically references these connectors when relevant, making it faster to bring Google workspace information into conversations without manual selection each time.
This expansion continues the rollout of connector functionality from Pro to Plus users, with Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans expected to follow in subsequent weeks.

title_updated: GPT-5: Add Auto, Fast & Thinking model selector with expanded rate limits
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers Auto, Fast, and Thinking options for GPT-5, with Plus users receiving 3,000 messages per week with GPT-5 Thinking and extra capacity on GPT-5 Thinking mini after that limit, with a 196k token context.
GPT-4o returns to the model picker for all paid users by default, with a Show additional models toggle in web settings adding o3, o4-mini, 4.1, and GPT-5 Thinking mini, while GPT-4.5 remains Pro-exclusive due to GPU constraints.
This update addresses user demand for more granular model control, moving away from the initial auto-only GPT-5 experience toward explicit selection options.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch Gmail, Google Calendar & Contacts for Pro users
content_updated:
Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts are now available as connectors in ChatGPT, automatically referencing relevant information from these tools in conversations once enabled by the user.
Rolling out to Pro users globally first, with Plus, Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans following in coming weeks. Users who already have Gmail or Google Calendar enabled for deep research can now also use them in chat.
This represents a significant expansion of ChatGPT's integration capabilities as part of GPT-5, enabling seamless access to personal productivity data without manually selecting connectors for each conversation.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Expand Box, Canva, Dropbox, HubSpot & more to Plus & Pro chat
content_updated:
ChatGPT expands connector availability for chat search, adding Box, Canva, Dropbox, HubSpot, Notion, Microsoft SharePoint, and Microsoft Teams for Plus users, and Microsoft Teams and GitHub for Pro users.
Rollout begins immediately with all eligible accounts gaining access in coming days. Connectors are enabled via Settings and remain in beta, defaulted off for Enterprise and Edu plans where admins can enable them.
Connectors for Plus and Pro plans are not available in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, with this expansion building on the deep research connectors that were previously the only way to access third-party data.

title_updated: ChatGPT Images: Improve generation efficiency for Free tier users
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out an update to image generation for ChatGPT Free tier users that makes performance more efficient, optimizing how images are generated without changing the user-facing feature set.
The update targets the backend efficiency of image generation specifically for free-tier users, likely reducing compute costs while maintaining output quality.
This efficiency improvement allows OpenAI to sustain image generation availability for free users as demand grows, following the broader ChatGPT Images launch earlier in the year.

title_updated: GPT-5: Launch flagship auto-switching model for all ChatGPT users worldwide
content_updated:
OpenAI launches GPT-5 in ChatGPT as the new default flagship model rolling out to Plus, Pro, Team, and Free plans worldwide across web, mobile, and desktop, with Enterprise and Edu plans following soon.
GPT-5 simplifies ChatGPT to a single auto-switching system combining the best of previous models, with paid users accessing the model picker for manual GPT-5 or GPT-5 Thinking selection, and Pro and Team users getting GPT-5 Thinking Pro for complex tasks.
GPT-5 represents a fundamental shift from multiple model choices to a unified intelligent system that automatically selects the best approach for each query.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch four personality options plus Default style
content_updated:
ChatGPT now offers four distinct personality options alongside the Default style in Customize settings: Cynic for sarcastic directness, Robot for precise efficiency, Listener for warm reflection, and Nerd for playful curiosity.
Each personality has its own communication style and tone that applies to text conversations, though personalities do not apply to Voice mode at launch.
This marks ChatGPT's first personality system, giving users the ability to choose how ChatGPT communicates beyond custom instructions, launched alongside the GPT-5 rollout.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch accent color customization for conversation UI
content_updated:
ChatGPT now lets users set an accent color that applies to conversation bubbles, the Voice button, highlighted text, and other UI elements across the interface.
On web, the setting is accessible via profile icon, Settings, General tab, and Accent color dropdown. On mobile, users navigate to profile, Personalization, then Color Scheme to select their preferred accent.
This visual customization option launches alongside GPT-5 and the personality system, expanding user personalization beyond conversation style to include the visual appearance of the interface.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Expand Advanced Voice access & announce Standard Voice retirement
content_updated:
ChatGPT expands Advanced Voice Mode with near-unlimited use for Plus users and hours per day for Free users, now supporting custom GPTs and paid-user instruction adaptation for style, speed, and tone.
Voice now adapts to user instructions for paid users, and Standard Voice Mode will be retired in 30 days to unify all users onto the latest voice experience.
This represents the most significant expansion of Voice Mode accessibility since its initial launch, moving from limited daily minutes to extensive daily hours while simplifying the product by phasing out the legacy Standard Voice.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Study Mode for interactive Socratic-style learning
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Study Mode, a learning experience that uses interactive Socratic-style questions to understand goals and skill level, then guides users to answers through progressive concept building and personalized feedback.
Available on Free, Plus, Pro, and Teams plans globally across iOS, Android, web, and desktop, Study Mode can be enabled via the Tools menu or chatgpt.com/studymode, and works with uploaded materials like images and PDFs.
Study Mode is powered by custom system instructions and may have inconsistent behavior across conversations, with plans to train the behavior directly into main models once iteration and user feedback reveal what works best.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add Canva & Notion for Pro users in chat & deep research
content_updated:
Pro users can now connect Canva and Notion for both chat search and deep research, expanding the connector ecosystem with two popular creative and productivity tools.
This feature is currently limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK due to regional regulatory considerations.
The addition continues the expansion of Pro-exclusive connector integrations, building on earlier Google workspace and Microsoft connectors to cover more of the tools knowledge workers use daily.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Add HubSpot & custom MCP connectors for Pro chat search
content_updated:
Pro users can now use chat search with HubSpot and custom connectors built via Model Context Protocol in addition to their existing deep research functionality.
This feature is limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, matching the regional restrictions of other connector features.
The MCP custom connector support in chat search is particularly significant, allowing Pro users to connect proprietary systems to ChatGPT's conversational interface beyond the pre-built integrations.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Roll out Advanced Voice improvements to Free tier users
content_updated:
The Advanced Voice upgrades announced on June 7 for paid users are now rolling out to ChatGPT Free users, delivering the same improvements in natural-sounding speech, expressiveness, and translation effectiveness.
Rate limits for Free users remain unchanged despite the quality improvements, maintaining the existing usage boundaries while upgrading the underlying voice experience.
This brings Free users to parity with paid users on Advanced Voice quality, approximately six weeks after the initial paid-user rollout of enhanced intonation, cadence, and emotion handling.

title_updated: ChatGPT macOS: Launch Record Mode for meeting transcription & summarization for Plus users
content_updated:
Record Mode is now available to ChatGPT Plus users globally in the macOS desktop app, allowing users to record live conversations like team meetings or voice notes and turn them into editable summaries in Canvas.
The feature is exclusive to the macOS desktop app and produces summaries that can be further edited and refined using Canvas's collaborative editing capabilities.
Record Mode originally rolled out to Team users on June 4 and Enterprise and Edu plans on June 18, with this Plus expansion completing the rollout across all paid tiers.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch chat search for Dropbox, Box, Google Drive & more for Pro users
content_updated:
Pro users can now use chat search connectors for Dropbox, Box, Google Drive (synced and non-synced), Microsoft OneDrive Business, and Microsoft SharePoint, in addition to existing deep research connector support.
This feature is limited to users located outside of the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK, and can be enabled from Connector settings in the application.
The expansion from deep-research-only to chat search means Pro users can now reference files from these cloud storage services in everyday conversations, not just during formal research sessions.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Increase file upload limit to 40 for Pro users
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects now support up to 40 uploaded files per project for Pro users, doubled from the previous limit of 20 files.
This increase applies to Pro users only and enables larger knowledge bases within individual projects for more comprehensive context in conversations.
The expanded limit supports power users who rely on Projects for document-heavy workflows like research, analysis, and content creation that require extensive reference material.

title_updated: ChatGPT Record: Launch meeting capture for Pro, Enterprise & Edu on macOS
content_updated:
ChatGPT Record Mode launches for Pro, Enterprise, and Edu users on the macOS desktop app, enabling capture of meetings, brainstorms, and voice notes with automatic transcription, summarization, and output generation.
The feature can produce follow-ups, plans, and even code from recorded audio, available exclusively on the macOS desktop app. Team users received access on June 4, 2025.
Record Mode represents ChatGPT's first ambient audio feature, extending the product beyond interactive chat into passive capture and processing of real-world conversations.

title_updated: Custom GPTs: Expand model selection to Enterprise & Edu users
content_updated:
Enterprise and Edu users can now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models including GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini, and more when building Custom GPTs, matching the model selection previously available to Plus, Pro, and Team users.
GPTs without Custom Actions can use the model picker to select from all available models, while GPTs with Custom Actions currently support only GPT-4o and GPT-4.1.
This expansion completes the rollout of multi-model Custom GPT support across all paid tiers, available on web for all paid plans.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Test event with pricing information
content_updated:
ChatGPT test event with pricing was announced with no additional details beyond a test summary.
No specific features, changes, or timeline were provided in the release note.
Refer to the source URL for any subsequent updates or clarifications.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Test event for new feature category
content_updated:
ChatGPT test event for new-only category was announced with no additional details beyond a test summary.
No specific features, changes, or timeline were provided in the release note.
Refer to the source URL for any subsequent updates or clarifications.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Test event for fix category
content_updated:
ChatGPT test event for fix-only category was announced with no additional details beyond a test summary.
No specific features, changes, or timeline were provided in the release note.
Refer to the source URL for any subsequent updates or clarifications.

title_updated: ChatGPT Search: Upgrade with multi-search, image search & instruction following
content_updated:
ChatGPT Search is upgraded for all users with smarter responses, improved understanding of queries, more comprehensive answers, and better handling of longer conversational contexts.
New capabilities include automatic multi-search for complex questions, image-based web search from uploaded photos, and more robust instruction following especially in longer conversations that significantly reduces repetitive responses.
Users may notice longer responses with this experience, and occasional unexpected chain-of-thought reasoning may appear for simple queries, with a fix rolling out shortly.

title_updated: Custom GPTs: Launch multi-model selection for Plus, Pro & Team creators
content_updated:
Custom GPT creators on Plus, Pro, and Team plans can now choose from the full set of ChatGPT models including GPT-4o, o3, o4-mini, and more when building GPTs, with the ability to set a recommended model for users.
GPTs without Custom Actions support the full model picker, while GPTs with Custom Actions currently support GPT-4o and GPT-4.1 only, available on web.
Enterprise and Edu rollout is coming soon, expanding creator control over which model powers their custom GPT for different tasks and workflows.

title_updated: ChatGPT Projects: Add deep research, voice mode, memory & mobile file uploads
content_updated:
ChatGPT Projects receive several updates for Plus, Pro, and Team users, including deep research and voice mode support within projects, improvements to memory for referencing past project chats, and the ability to share chats from projects.
Additional updates include starting new projects directly from a chat, uploading files on mobile, and accessing the model selector on mobile. Memory improvements are limited to Plus and Pro users.
This update transforms Projects from a basic organizational tool into a more capable workspace with access to ChatGPT's most powerful features directly within the project context.

title_updated: o3-pro: Launch highest-reliability reasoning model for Pro & Team users
content_updated:
OpenAI launches o3-pro for Pro and Team users in ChatGPT, a version of the o3 model designed to think longer and provide the most reliable responses, replacing o1-pro in the model picker with Enterprise and Edu access following.
In expert evaluations, o3-pro is consistently preferred over o3 in every tested category and outperforms both o1-pro and o3 on academic benchmarks using rigorous 4/4 reliability scoring where all four attempts must be correct.
Unlike o1-pro, o3-pro has access to all ChatGPT tools including web search, file analysis, Python, memory, and visual reasoning, though responses typically take longer and temporary chats, image generation, and Canvas are not currently supported.

title_updated: ChatGPT Voice: Upgrade Advanced Voice with natural intonation & real-time translation
content_updated:
Advanced Voice Mode receives a major upgrade for all paid users with significantly enhanced intonation, realistic cadence with natural pauses and emphases, and more expressive emotion handling including empathy, sarcasm, and more.
Voice now offers intuitive real-time language translation that persists throughout conversations, enabling use cases like restaurant conversations abroad or multilingual business meetings with seamless back-and-forth translation.
Available across all platforms for paid users, with known limitations including occasional audio quality variations with certain voice options and rare hallucinations producing unintended sounds resembling ads, gibberish, or background music.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch beta deep research integration for all paid plans
content_updated:
ChatGPT connectors launch in beta for deep research across Team, Enterprise, Edu, Pro, and Plus plans globally, enabling long-form cited responses that combine internal tools with web sources.
Supported connectors include Google Drive, SharePoint, Dropbox, Box, Outlook, Gmail, Google Calendar, Linear, GitHub, HubSpot, and Teams, with Pro and Plus users excluding those in Switzerland, EEA, and the UK.
This marks the first time ChatGPT can synthesize information from enterprise tools alongside web sources in deep research, bridging the gap between public knowledge and private organizational data.

title_updated: ChatGPT Connectors: Launch custom MCP connectors for Pro, Team, Enterprise & Edu
content_updated:
Admins and users can now build and deploy custom connectors to proprietary systems using Model Context Protocol, requiring a remote MCP server and currently available only in deep research.
Admin-published connectors appear in the connector list for all workspace users, with Team, Enterprise, and Edu plans restricting custom connector creation to admins only.
MCP custom connectors enable organizations to connect ChatGPT to internal systems beyond the pre-built integrations, opening deep research to proprietary databases, APIs, and tools specific to each organization.

title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Expand comprehensive memory with chat history to Free users
content_updated:
ChatGPT's enhanced memory improvements begin rolling out to Free users, adding conversation history reference alongside existing saved memories to deliver more relevant and tailored responses.
Free users must be logged in with up-to-date apps, and users in the EEA, Switzerland, and related regions must explicitly opt in via Settings, while users elsewhere receive the upgrade automatically if memory is enabled.
This expansion brings Free users closer to the memory experience available to Plus and Pro users since May 2025, making ChatGPT's long-term personalization accessible across all tiers.

title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add Dropbox connector for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with Dropbox, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
Once connected, deep research can access and reference Dropbox files alongside web sources to generate more comprehensive and contextually relevant research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, continuing the phased connector rollout that started with Google Drive and SharePoint earlier in 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Launch GitHub connector globally for Plus, Pro & Team
content_updated:
The GitHub connector for ChatGPT deep research is now available globally to Plus, Pro, and Team users, including those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
This makes GitHub the first deep research connector available without regional restrictions, enabling developers to reference repository data directly in research reports.
The global availability contrasts with other connectors like Dropbox and SharePoint that exclude EEA and UK users, suggesting GitHub met different regulatory requirements for unrestricted deployment.

title_updated: GPT-4.1: Launch in ChatGPT for all paid users as specialized coding model
content_updated:
GPT-4.1, previously available only in the API since April, is now accessible in ChatGPT for Plus, Pro, and Team users via the more models dropdown, with Enterprise and Edu access coming in following weeks.
GPT-4.1 excels at coding tasks with stronger instruction following and web development capabilities compared to GPT-4o, offering an alternative to o3 and o4-mini for simpler everyday coding needs with the same rate limits as GPT-4o.
This brings a developer-favorite API model to the ChatGPT consumer product by popular demand, bridging the gap between API and chat model availability.

title_updated: GPT-4.1 mini: Launch as GPT-4o mini replacement for all ChatGPT users
content_updated:
GPT-4.1 mini launches as a fast, capable small model replacing GPT-4o mini in the ChatGPT model picker, delivering significant improvements in instruction following, coding, and overall intelligence.
For paid users, GPT-4.1 mini appears under more models, while for free users it serves as the fallback model once GPT-4o usage limits are reached, with rate limits remaining unchanged.
Safety evaluations for both GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini are available in the newly launched Safety Evaluations Hub, providing transparency into model safety testing.

title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add SharePoint & OneDrive connector for Plus, Pro & Team
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with Microsoft SharePoint and OneDrive, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
The connector enables deep research to access and reference documents stored in SharePoint and OneDrive alongside web sources for more comprehensive enterprise-oriented research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, with this Microsoft integration complementing the existing Google Drive connector for deep research.

title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Launch PDF export for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research reports can now be exported as well-formatted PDFs complete with tables, images, linked citations, and sources, available for both new and past reports.
Users click the share icon and select Download as PDF to generate the export, providing a professional-quality document suitable for sharing and archiving.
This addresses the need to share deep research findings outside of ChatGPT, enabling users to distribute reports as standalone documents with proper formatting and citation attribution.

title_updated: ChatGPT Deep Research: Add GitHub connector for Plus, Pro & Team users
content_updated:
ChatGPT deep research now integrates with GitHub, available globally to Team users and gradually rolling out to Plus and Pro users except those in the EEA, Switzerland, and the UK.
The connector enables deep research to access and reference GitHub repositories, issues, and code alongside web sources for more comprehensive developer-oriented research reports.
Enterprise user access will be announced at a later date, with GitHub joining Google Drive, SharePoint, and Dropbox as available deep research data sources.

title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Launch enhanced memory with chat history for EU Plus & Pro users
content_updated:
Enhanced memory featuring chat history reference rolls out to all Plus and Pro users globally, including the EEA, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein where the features are off by default and must be manually enabled.
Plus and Pro accounts get both saved memories and chat history, while Free-tier users have access to saved memories only. Users outside European regions who have memory enabled receive the upgrade automatically.
This marks a significant milestone as the first time ChatGPT's enhanced memory features are available in the EU and related regions, requiring explicit opt-in to comply with European data protection requirements.

title_updated: ChatGPT Mobile: Consolidate tool icons into Skills menu on iOS & Android
content_updated:
ChatGPT removes the row of individual tool icons from the mobile composer on iOS and Android, replacing them with a single sliders-style icon that opens a new Skills menu bottom sheet.
No tools are deprecated in this change; access to features like Create an image and Search the web is simply consolidated into the Skills menu to clear space and reduce on-screen clutter.
This UI simplification affects Free, Plus, and Pro plans and reflects the growing number of ChatGPT tools that can no longer fit as individual icons in the mobile composer.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Sunset Monday personality after one-month surprise run
content_updated:
OpenAI sunsets the Monday personality, an irreverent text and voice personality that launched on April 1 as a planned one-month surprise feature, with new personalities already in development.
The personality was available in both voice and text modes during its limited run, offering a distinctly different tone from ChatGPT's standard communication style.
Monday served as an experimental personality feature that preceded the broader personality system launched with GPT-5 in August 2025, with user feedback likely informing the design of subsequent personality options.

title_updated: GPT-4o: Revert update due to sycophancy issues with overly agreeable responses
content_updated:
OpenAI reverts the most recent GPT-4o update due to issues with overly agreeable responses identified as sycophancy, where the model provided excessive validation rather than honest, direct answers.
The revert restores the previous GPT-4o behavior while OpenAI works on further improvements, with detailed explanations published in blog posts about what happened and planned changes.
This incident highlighted the challenge of balancing conversational warmth with honesty in language models, leading to OpenAI's public commitment to addressing sycophancy as a systematic research priority.

title_updated: GPT-4o: Improve memory optimization, STEM problem-solving & conversational guidance
content_updated:
OpenAI makes additional improvements to GPT-4o, optimizing when it saves memories, enhancing problem-solving for STEM tasks, and making it more proactive at guiding conversations toward productive outcomes.
Subtle response style changes make GPT-4o more intuitive and effective across a variety of tasks, with the model becoming better at steering interactions constructively rather than passively responding.
These updates represent the latest in a series of GPT-4o refinements, following the sycophancy revert in late April and preceding the GPT-5 launch in August 2025.

title_updated: o3 & o4-mini: Launch reasoning models with full tool access in ChatGPT
content_updated:
OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini as the latest o-series reasoning models in ChatGPT, the smartest models to date, with the first-ever ability to agentically use and combine all ChatGPT tools including web search, file analysis, Python, visual reasoning, and image generation.
The models are trained to reason about when and how to use tools, typically responding in under a minute, with significantly stronger performance across academic benchmarks and real-world tasks for multi-faceted questions.
This marks a step toward a more agentic ChatGPT that can independently execute tasks, combining state-of-the-art reasoning with full tool access to set a new standard in both intelligence and usefulness.

title_updated: ChatGPT Memory: Enable memory-informed web search queries
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now use saved memories to inform search queries when performing web searches through third-party search providers, making search results more relevant to the user's context and preferences.
This integration means search queries are automatically enriched with relevant personal context from memories, without requiring users to manually include background information in their prompts.
The feature builds on ChatGPT's memory system to bridge the gap between personalized conversation and web search, enabling more contextually relevant results.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch Image Library for browsing & reusing generated images
content_updated:
All images created with ChatGPT are now automatically saved to a new Library in the sidebar, providing one place to browse, revisit, and reuse generated images without searching through past conversations.
Rolling out on Web, iOS, and Android for Free, Plus, and Pro users, the Library currently displays 4o-generated images while older creations are backfilled, with deletion available by removing the source conversation.
Enterprise and Edu support is coming soon, making this one of the first ChatGPT features to launch with a dedicated content management interface outside of conversation history.
