title_updated: GPT-4: Announce retirement from ChatGPT effective April 30, replaced by GPT-4o
content_updated:
OpenAI announces that GPT-4 will be retired from ChatGPT on April 30, 2025, with GPT-4o serving as the full replacement, while GPT-4 remains available in the API.
GPT-4o consistently surpasses GPT-4 in head-to-head evaluations across writing, coding, STEM, and more, with recent upgrades further improving instruction following, problem solving, and conversational flow.
GPT-4 marked a pivotal moment in ChatGPT's evolution, and its retirement represents the natural progression as GPT-4o builds on that foundation with greater capability, consistency, and creativity.

title_updated: GPT-4o: Improve creativity, coding, STEM & instruction following
content_updated:
GPT-4o receives a major update making it more intuitive, creative, and collaborative, with enhanced instruction following, smarter coding that generates cleaner frontend code, and more accurate STEM problem-solving.
The model better understands implied intent behind prompts for creative tasks, produces code that compiles and runs more reliably, follows complex multi-part instructions more accurately, and delivers slightly more concise responses with fewer markdown hierarchies and emojis.
This update is available in ChatGPT and the API as the newest chatgpt-4o-latest snapshot, with plans to bring these improvements to a dated API model in the coming weeks.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Announce usability & performance improvements across Web, Android & iOS
content_updated:
ChatGPT announces a variety of usability and performance improvements and bug fixes across Web, Android, and iOS platforms with a small number of specific callouts.
No specific features, changes, or timeline were provided beyond the general announcement in the release note.
For updates on the ChatGPT macOS app, users are directed to a separate page, suggesting platform-specific release notes are maintained independently.

title_updated: ChatGPT Web & Windows: Add inline error retries, o1/o3-mini data analysis & draft saving
content_updated:
ChatGPT Web and Windows desktop app receive four updates: inline message error retries that let users retry or continue chatting, Python-powered data analysis for o1 and o3-mini models, conversation draft saving for unsubmitted messages, and a new UI for Temporary Chats.
The o1 and o3-mini data analysis enables tasks like running regressions, visualizing business metrics, and conducting scenario-based simulations directly within conversations.
These quality-of-life improvements address common friction points including lost messages after errors, limited model capabilities for data work, and disappearing draft messages.

title_updated: ChatGPT Android: Increase default image size & add incognito keyboard for Temporary Chats
content_updated:
ChatGPT Android app receives two updates: increased default size for inline generated images making them more visible, and a more clear and private incognito keyboard for Temporary Chats.
The larger image display improves the visual experience when viewing AI-generated images within conversations, while the incognito keyboard adds privacy for temporary chat sessions.
These Android-specific improvements are part of a broader cross-platform update wave covering Web, Windows, Android, and iOS released on March 18, 2025.

title_updated: ChatGPT iOS: Improve table copying, add long-press menus & faster streaming
content_updated:
ChatGPT iOS app receives four updates: improved table-copying functionality for iOS and macOS, long-press on messages to access Copy and Edit actions, improved nested blockquote display, and faster conversation streaming with message menu actions.
These improvements focus on content interaction and display quality, making it easier to extract, navigate, and read complex formatted content in chat responses.
The updates are part of a broader cross-platform release covering Web, Windows, Android, and iOS on March 18, 2025, with iOS receiving the most interaction-focused improvements.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch GPT-4.5 for Pro users & improve temporary chat accessibility
content_updated:
GPT-4.5 is released to Pro plan users in ChatGPT, and the temporary chat icon moves to the top bar for improved accessibility and clarity across the interface.
iOS receives long-press text selection, always-visible message quick actions, bug fixes in the 1.2025.056 release, and an opt-in setting for auto-submitting transcriptions. Android receives improved error handling for Play Integrity errors.
This bundled release combines a major model launch for Pro users with cross-platform UX improvements targeting everyday interaction quality.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Launch iOS widget, text selection fixes & performance improvements
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces an iOS widget alongside text selection improvements that now support selection across horizontal rulers and performance improvements for faster model header loading and conversation history display.
A bug fix resolves 401 errors that were logging out users on Android when dismissing tooltips, and GPT display shows up faster across the interface.
This release focuses on platform-specific quality improvements, with the iOS widget being the headline feature for quick ChatGPT access from the home screen.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Add Safari search engine option, logout confirmation & dictation preview
content_updated:
ChatGPT can now be set as the default search engine in Safari, and a new confirmation dialog prevents accidental logouts on iOS and web.
Additional updates include improved Android conversation parsing performance, iOS performance fixes, updated Android launch animations and composer design, and a voice-to-text dictation preview showing transcribed text after recording.
The Safari search engine integration marks ChatGPT's entry into the browser search market, competing directly with traditional search engines for default search position.

title_updated: ChatGPT Canvas: Launch sharing for rendered code, documents & code assets
content_updated:
ChatGPT Canvas now supports sharing assets including rendered React and HTML code, documents, and code with other users, similar to existing conversation sharing functionality.
Sharing is accessible from the Canvas toolbar when Canvas is open, enabling direct collaboration on Canvas-created outputs without requiring recipients to recreate the content.
This extends Canvas from a personal creation tool into a collaborative platform where users can share interactive code previews, formatted documents, and code snippets with others.

title_updated: ChatGPT: Update custom instructions UI with traits, communication style & rules
content_updated:
ChatGPT updates custom instructions with a new UI that makes it easier to specify desired traits, communication style, and rules for ChatGPT to follow, without changing existing custom instruction settings.
The new UI is rolling out on chatgpt.com and Windows desktop first, with mobile and macOS support coming in the following weeks, and will be available soon for EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Switzerland users.
This redesign makes personalization more structured and accessible compared to the previous freeform text approach, guiding users to specify preferences across distinct categories.

title_updated: ChatGPT Canvas: Launch import from chat for editing responses & code blocks
content_updated:
ChatGPT Canvas now lets users import and edit full model responses or individual code blocks from chat directly into the Canvas editor, available on web only.
This feature enables users to take any ChatGPT response and move it into Canvas for in-place editing, refinement, and collaboration without manual copy-pasting.
Desktop and mobile support are not included in this initial release, with Canvas import functionality being web-exclusive as the feature launches.

title_updated: ChatGPT Tasks: Launch scheduled automated prompts in beta for Plus, Pro & Teams
content_updated:
ChatGPT introduces Tasks in early beta, a new capability for scheduling one-time reminders or recurring automated prompts that ChatGPT executes at specified future times.
The feature is available for Plus, Pro, and Teams plans, with plans to eventually make it available to anyone with a ChatGPT account.
Scheduled Tasks represents ChatGPT's first asynchronous automation feature, moving beyond real-time conversation toward proactive task execution on the user's behalf.

title_updated: ChatGPT in CarPlay: Hands-free voice access while driving on iOS 26.4+
content_updated:
ChatGPT rolls out in Apple CarPlay, giving users hands-free access through their car's CarPlay interface for voice conversations on the go.
Requires iOS 26.4 or newer and a supported car connection; users can start new voice conversations from CarPlay and resume existing voice mode conversations from the ChatGPT mobile app.
This brings ChatGPT into the in-vehicle experience for the first time, complementing the broader voice mode rollout and positioning ChatGPT alongside Siri and Google Assistant for hands-free assistance during driving.

title_updated: Outlook Apps: Add support for shared mailboxes & delegated calendars in ChatGPT
content_updated:
The Outlook Email and Calendar apps for ChatGPT now help with delegated or shared Outlook resources, letting ChatGPT read, send, and manage mail or events on behalf of those mailboxes.
Shared mailbox support covers reading messages, browsing folders, marking read/unread, moving messages, and sending plain-text mail; shared calendars allow create, update, RSVP, cancel, delete, and add small attachments.
Users must connect the Outlook Email or Calendar app and provide the exact shared mailbox address or calendar; availability depends on Microsoft permissions and workspace app settings, expanding ChatGPT's enterprise productivity reach.

title_updated: Pro Plans: New $100/month tier with up to 10x Codex usage of Plus
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces a new $100/month Pro plan and updates how Codex usage works across Plus and Pro tiers, balancing high-intensity sessions with steady daily use.
Pro $100/mo includes unlimited GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4 Pro access, and up to 10x Codex usage of Plus (up from 5x); Pro $200/mo continues current Codex promotion through May 31; Plus $20/mo rebalances Codex for steady weekly sessions.
This restructures the Pro tier to capture users who want intense Codex sessions without the $200 premium, while ending the temporary Codex promotion on Plus to spread usage across the week rather than single high-intensity days.

title_updated: GPT-5.3 Instant Mini: Fallback model after GPT-5.3 Instant rate limits
content_updated:
OpenAI releases GPT-5.3 Instant Mini in ChatGPT, replacing GPT-5 Instant Mini as the fallback model users reach after hitting their GPT-5.3 Instant rate limits.
GPT-5.3 Instant Mini won't appear in the model picker because it serves as a fallback only; it outperforms GPT-5 Instant Mini with more natural conversation, stronger writing, and better contextual awareness.
This refreshes the rate-limit fallback layer to match the GPT-5.3 series, ensuring users hitting limits get a more capable mini model rather than dropping back to the older GPT-5 generation.

title_updated: Ads: Rollout begins for Free & Go plans in Australia, New Zealand & Canada
content_updated:
ChatGPT begins rolling out ads to users on Free and Go plans in Australia, New Zealand, and Canada as part of a phased monetization expansion.
Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education plans do not include ads; the rollout is limited to the three named markets at this stage with no announced timeline for expansion.
This continues OpenAI's push to monetize free-tier ChatGPT usage in non-U.S. markets, preserving an ad-free experience as a paid-plan benefit and signaling further geographic ad rollouts to come.

title_updated: ChatGPT Images 2.0: New image generation model with images-with-thinking on paid plans
content_updated:
OpenAI introduces ChatGPT Images 2.0, a new image generation model in ChatGPT, alongside images with thinking that plans and refines outputs before generating them.
ChatGPT Images 2.0 is available on all ChatGPT plans; images with thinking is available on all paid plans when Thinking and Pro models are selected from the model picker.
This succeeds the original 4o Image Generation, layering deliberate planning into image generation for higher-quality outputs on paid tiers while keeping the base model accessible to free users.

title_updated: ChatGPT for Clinicians: Free workspace for verified U.S. clinicians with CME credits
content_updated:
OpenAI launches ChatGPT for Clinicians, a free version of ChatGPT for verified clinicians in the United States designed to support real clinical work at the time of care.
Includes evidence review, documentation, medical research, trusted clinical search with citations, reusable skills, deep research across medical literature, and CME credit support for eligible clinical questions.
Existing ChatGPT users can sign up with their same account; ChatGPT for Clinicians appears as a separate workspace under the same login while the existing workspace remains available, marking OpenAI's first vertical-specific ChatGPT product for medical professionals.

title_updated: Fast answers: Quicker responses for common information-seeking questions
content_updated:
ChatGPT rolls out Fast answers, a quicker way to get responses to common information-seeking questions when no personalization is required and ChatGPT has a high-confidence answer ready.
Available globally on web, iOS, and Android for logged-in and logged-out users across all plans; Fast answers do not reference past chats or memory and can be turned off in Personalization settings.
This trades personalization for speed on factual lookups like the Seven Wonders or Super Bowl champions, with planned expansion to more question types over time as a complement to standard personalized responses.

title_updated: GPT-5.5 in ChatGPT: Smartest frontier model for professional work & agentic coding
content_updated:
OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 in ChatGPT as the smartest frontier model for professional work, with stronger goal understanding, tool use, and self-checking to carry tasks through to completion.
GPT-5.5 improves on agentic coding (terminal workflows, GitHub issue resolution, long-horizon tasks), professional knowledge work, and document-grounded retrieval; GPT-5.5 Thinking is on eligible paid plans, GPT-5.5 Pro on Pro/Business/Enterprise/Edu.
This is a major step forward from GPT-5, expanding ChatGPT's ability to handle messy multi-step requests across coding, research, analysis, and document creation with stronger reasoning and higher-confidence outputs.
